messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] dynamically changing the number of ODEs
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] dynamically changing the number of ODEs
messages.body_plain:
Dear Antoine,

I think I understand what you mean, but this is not possible by 
definition, i.e. solving solve a "system" of differential equations.

The technical reason is, that the algorithm needs to know how much 
memory is to be allocated for internal data structures and the
simulation results at the beginning.

You can, however, define "dummy" state variables that are dynamically
brought to life. Another option would be to chain deSolve models in
a simulation loop, e.g. to embed an ODE system in an event loop of
an individual-based model.

Thomas

-- 
Dr. Thomas Petzoldt
Technische Universitaet Dresden
Faculty of Environmental Sciences
Institute of Hydrobiology
01062 Dresden, Germany

http://tu-dresden.de/Members/thomas.petzoldt

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] useR!2014 in LA: abstract submission until 2014-03-30
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] useR!2014 in LA: abstract submission until 2014-03-30
messages.body_plain:
Dear dynamic modellers,

you may have noticed that the useR!2014, the main meeting of the R user
and developer community, will take place in Los Angeles this year from:

June 30 to July 3 2014

The is being organized with support from the UCLA Statistics Department:

http://user2014.stat.ucla.edu/

... the origin and maintainers of the Journal of Statistical Software
and many other contributions to free and open source statistics software.

I am absolutely sure that it will become an outstandingly big and
exciting event! It is also a unique chance to share ideas and experience
in the field of dynamic modelling. Karline Soetaert will give a keynote
about "Solving Differential Equations", we will have a tutorial on this
topic, and -- it would be really great to see what people do in related
fields.

Please consider to attend and to submit an abstract for a talk or poster.

The submission deadline is approaching soon, at 2014-03-30, so don't
wait with your decision!

See you in LA!

Thomas
(R-sig-dyn-mod list moderator)


-- 
Dr. Thomas Petzoldt
Technische Universitaet Dresden
Faculty of Environmental Sciences
Institute of Hydrobiology
01062 Dresden, Germany

E-Mail: thomas.petzoldt at tu-dresden.de
http://tu-dresden.de/Members/thomas.petzoldt
